sourceMovie.title: You Don't Know Jack
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit You Don't Know Jack for the same reason it broke through in 2010: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a tv movie rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Barry Levinson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and tv movie blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Just Mercy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If You Don't Know Jack clicked for you, Just Mercy (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after You Don't Know Jack?
faq.answer: Try Just Mercy, October Sky, Antwone Fisher—each shares DNA with You Don't Know Jack while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is You Don't Know Jack?
faq.answer: You Don't Know Jack (2010) is best described as Drama, TV Movie, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is You Don't Know Jack worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
